asset.db.riskHealthyBody: No broken references, Missing Script, parse failures, or duplicate GUIDs need attention.

settings.title: Settings
settings.console.title: Console
settings.console.desc: View unified frontend and backend debug output. Backend debug and trace logs follow General > Debug Mode.
settings.console.searchPlaceholder: Filter by module or log text
settings.console.empty: No logs match the current filters.
settings.provider.title: Third-Party API Access
settings.provider.openrouter.desc: Unified gateway for Claude, GPT, Gemini, and more
settings.provider.anthropic_sdk.desc: Runs via the local Claude Code CLI using the Anthropic Agent SDK control protocol
settings.anthropic.title: Claude Subscription (Paused)

settings.anthropicSdk.title: Claude Agent SDK (Local CLI)
settings.anthropicSdk.hint: This channel runs the Agent SDK control protocol through the local Claude CLI, independent of the OAuth session.
settings.anthropicSdk.installHint: Install @anthropic-ai/claude-code and make sure the claude command is available in PATH.
settings.codex.title: ChatGPT Subscription (Device Auth)
settings.codex.desc: Invoke GPT-5.5 with a ChatGPT Pro / Plus subscription
settings.codex.hint: Compatible with ChatGPT Pro / Plus plans
settings.codex.instruction: Visit the link in browser, enter the code to authorize
settings.codex.validationFailedHint: Login info is preserved and needs validation
settings.codex.transportDesc: HTTP uses the current ChatGPT Codex path. WebSocket uses the OpenAI Responses websocket path.
settings.codex.transportHttpHint: Use the current ChatGPT Codex HTTP SSE path
settings.codex.transportWebsocketHint: Use the OpenAI Responses websocket path
settings.models.noModels: No models available. Configure a provider in API Access first.
settings.models.title: Default Model Config
settings.models.desc: Specify default models for different scenarios. When not set, follows current manual selection.
settings.models.mainHint: Default model used for the main chat session
settings.models.planHint: Model auto-used when switching to Plan mode
settings.models.subagentDesc: Per-subagent model overrides used when an Agent dispatches sub-tasks. When not set, sub-agents inherit the current session model.
settings.custom.title: Custom Model Endpoints
settings.custom.desc: Add custom API endpoints supporting OpenAI Chat Completions, OpenAI Responses, and Anthropic Messages formats.
settings.custom.name: Display Name
settings.custom.apiModelHint: The model field value sent to the API
settings.custom.endpointHint: Base URL with /v1, paths like /chat/completions are appended automatically
settings.custom.apiKeyOptional: Optional, some local models don't require a key
settings.custom.contextLengthHint: Token count, triggers context compaction when exceeded
settings.custom.betaFlagsHint: Anthropic beta headers to include in requests
settings.custom.testHtmlResponse: Server returned an HTML page instead of JSON, possibly a verification or challenge page.
settings.custom.testMissingFields: Please fill in API model ID and endpoint URL first
settings.perms.title: Tool Permissions
settings.perms.desc: Set the global tool execution mode and per-tool confirmation rules for Ask mode.
settings.perms.globalModeDesc: Auto runs all tools automatically; Ask uses the rules below.
settings.about.title: About
settings.about.desc: View product details and contact information.
settings.shortcuts.title: Shortcuts
settings.shortcuts.desc: Configure key combinations for common actions.
settings.shortcuts.sendModeDesc: Choose how Enter and {0}+Enter behave in the chat composer.
settings.shortcuts.newChatDesc: Start a new session immediately from the sessions view.
settings.shortcuts.captureHint: Click Record, then press a key combination. Include at least Ctrl, Alt, Shift, or Cmd. Press Esc to cancel.
settings.shortcuts.requireModifier: A shortcut must include at least one modifier key.
settings.display.themeDesc: Choose separate color styles for the main window and Unity embedded window
settings.display.panelBehaviorDesc: Control auto open/close behavior for TODO and file changes panels
settings.display.todoAutoOpen: Auto-open TODO panel when todos arrive
settings.display.changesAutoOpen: Auto-open file changes panel when changes arrive
settings.display.changesAutoClose: Auto-close file changes panel when sending a new message
settings.display.rightAlignUserMessages: Right-align user messages in the session view
settings.display.mergeGitTreeStatusIcon: Use colored icons for Git tree status
settings.display.notificationsDesc: Send desktop notifications for key chat events when the app is not focused
settings.display.fontDesc: Customize fonts for each area. Leave empty to use the default font stack
notifications.toolConfirmFallback: A tool action is waiting for approval
notifications.knowledgeProposalFallback: A knowledge proposal is waiting for review
settings.knowledge.unifiedDesc: This panel shows the unified Knowledge root and current document distribution for the workspace. Editing, search, and maintenance now live in the Knowledge view.
settings.knowledge.rootHint: The unified Knowledge store keeps only four top-level types: design, memory, skill, and reference. Each document maps to one Markdown file.
settings.knowledge.emptyRecent: No documents under the current type yet.
settings.knowledge.skillDesc: Manage skills in the Knowledge panel. Enable, disable, or configure individual skills from the Skill category.
settings.knowledge.knowledgeDesc: When enabled, agents can query documents in the project knowledge base
settings.knowledge.embeddingDesc: Enable vector search for more accurate results
settings.knowledge.embeddingModalHint: Turning it off keeps the current configuration but excludes it from semantic retrieval and index builds.
settings.knowledge.embeddingRequiresKnow: Enable Knowledge in the Knowledge view before configuring vector search.
settings.knowledge.deviceGpuDirectmlHint: Run the local embedding model with the DirectML backend.
settings.knowledge.deviceGpuCudaHint: Run the local embedding model with the CUDA backend.
settings.knowledge.customModelHint: Preset models auto-download on activation. Other model IDs should have ONNX files placed in this directory:
settings.knowledge.localUnavailable: Local embedding runtime is not implemented yet. Use remote mode.
settings.knowledge.localUnavailableHint: Local embeddings are not wired up yet; use a remote OpenAI-compatible embeddings endpoint for now.
settings.knowledge.localRuntimeHint: The local runtime uses fastembed. Supported preset models download automatically on first activation.
settings.knowledge.testMissingFields: Please fill in endpoint and model first
settings.knowledge.modelAutoDownload: Model will download automatically on first activation
settings.knowledge.modelNotDownloaded: Model not downloaded — place ONNX model files in the models directory
settings.general.resetOnboardingDesc: Clear the current configuration and re-enter the initial onboarding flow
settings.general.resetOnboardingConfirm: Reset all settings now? You will enter the onboarding wizard immediately.
settings.general.debugModeDesc: When enabled, every LLM HTTP request is saved to the debug capture directory. Dev builds use debug/llm/ and packaged builds use data/debug/llm/. Sensitive headers like Authorization are automatically redacted.
settings.general.storageDesc: Sessions and memory files are stored here, while Knowledge content stays in the project directory. You can open the current location or move it to a new one. The move finishes after restart.
settings.general.storagePendingDesc: After restart, the existing local data will be moved here.
settings.general.storagePendingRestart: The new location has been saved. Restart the app to start moving data.
settings.general.storagePendingCleared: The pending move has been cleared.
settings.general.storageUnavailable: Storage details are temporarily unavailable. Refresh and try again.
settings.general.pythonRuntimeDesc: The bash tool uses this interpreter when commands call python or python3.
settings.general.gitRuntimeDesc: Locus uses this Git for version control, undo, and change analysis.
settings.general.gitMissingSelected: The previously selected Git is unavailable. Locus is using an available runtime.
settings.general.gitNoRuntime: No available Git runtime was detected.
settings.general.pythonMissingSelected: The previously selected Python is unavailable. Locus is using an available runtime.
settings.general.pythonNoRuntime: No available Python runtime was detected.
knowledge.title: Knowledge Base
knowledge.explorer.title: Knowledge
knowledge.explorer.deleteFolderConfirm: Delete folder "{0}" and all its contents?
knowledge.explorer.name: Name
knowledge.explorer.subtitle: Browse the workspace knowledge documents by type
knowledge.explorer.empty: No documents in this type yet
knowledge.empty.title: Nothing selected
knowledge.empty.hint: Select a document from the left list, or use the search bar to find design, memory, skill, and reference content.
knowledge.meta.title: Metadata
knowledge.meta.inheritParentHint: Use the parent folder config. Top-level folders use the type default.
knowledge.meta.editMode.inheritParentHin: Use the parent folder AI edit config. Top-level documents use the type default.
knowledge.meta.editMode.readOnlyHint: View only, with manual mode switching.
knowledge.meta.editMode.proposalHint: Apply document changes after proposal confirmation.
knowledge.meta.editMode.autoHint: Let the agent update it under maintenance rules.
knowledge.meta.explicitMaintenanceRulesH: Keep a dedicated maintenance rules section in the document to constrain future updates.
knowledge.meta.rulesRequiredHint: Maintenance rules are required for automatic editing.
knowledge.meta.inject.noneHint: Available for search and recall without proactive context injection.
knowledge.meta.inject.pathHint: L0. Provide the title and path in context, then read the body on demand.
knowledge.meta.inject.excerptHint: L1. Provide the summary in context, or fall back to the opening body snippet.
knowledge.meta.inject.fullHint: L2. Inject maintenance rules and the full body as always-on knowledge in the Knowledge L2 Memory section.
knowledge.meta.inject.ruleHint: L3. Inject both maintenance rules and the full document body as active rules for high-priority long-term constraints.
knowledge.defaults.rules.design:
- Record confirmed design decisions and constraints
- Keep open questions current and remove outdated approaches
- Preserve the existing document structure while updating details
knowledge.defaults.rules.memory:
- Keep only durable and reusable project memory
- Consolidate duplicates or conflicts into the latest conclusion
- Remove temporary context, one-off tasks, and unsupported guesses
knowledge.defaults.rules.skill:
- Keep workflow steps, checks, and outputs stable
- Update key steps when tools or process changes
- Remove obsolete commands, repeated notes, and expired limits
knowledge.defaults.rules.reference:
- Capture verifiable facts, interfaces, and constraints
- Prefer durable conclusions and version-specific differences
- Remove outdated examples, repeated summaries, and unverified details
knowledge.preview.titleInvalid: Document name cannot contain path separators or ..
knowledge.preview.summaryHint: Optional. Used for excerpt injection and search previews
knowledge.preview.summaryPlaceholder: Leave empty when no summary is needed.
knowledge.preview.rulesHint: Shown when maintenance rules are enabled and required in automatic mode
knowledge.preview.rulesInheritedHint: These rules come from the parent folder or type default. Switch to a local config to edit them.
knowledge.preview.rulesPlaceholder: Describe the constraints and preserved content for automatic maintenance.
knowledge.preview.bodyHint: Primary document content stored in the same Markdown file
knowledge.directoryConfig.title: Folder Config
knowledge.directoryConfig.summaryPlaceho: Describe what this folder maintains and which structural facts it covers
knowledge.directoryConfig.injectModeHint: Controls how much folder context stays in the structure index. L1 appends the folder summary after the path.
knowledge.directoryConfig.inject.noneHin: Keep the folder in the knowledge tree and search without injecting its summary.
knowledge.directoryConfig.inject.pathHin: L0. Provide the folder path so the agent can locate the structure entry.
knowledge.directoryConfig.inject.excerpt: L1. Provide the folder path and summary for stable structure cache entry points.
knowledge.directoryConfig.aiConfigHint: Controls who maintains this folder cache and whether it follows the parent folder rules.
knowledge.directoryConfig.aiConfig.manua: You confirm proposals before folder config and cache changes apply.
knowledge.directoryConfig.aiConfig.autoH: Let the agent maintain the folder config and cache under maintenance rules.
knowledge.directoryConfig.aiMaintainedHi: Allow the agent to maintain this folder and its structure cache under these rules
knowledge.directoryConfig.explicitMainte: Keep dedicated maintenance rules for this folder to constrain cache updates and structural changes.
knowledge.directoryConfig.inheritToChild: Child folders and documents inherit this folder rule by default
knowledge.directoryConfig.allowCreateDoc: Allow creating new knowledge documents under this folder when facts need to be split out
knowledge.directoryConfig.allowCreateDir: Allow creating child folders when the structure needs to be reorganized
knowledge.directoryConfig.allowMoveDocum: Allow moving existing documents into more appropriate locations
knowledge.directoryConfig.allowMoveDirec: Allow rearranging child folder structure when needed
knowledge.directoryConfig.maintenanceRul: Folder-level rules used by the agent to maintain the structure cache in this subtree
knowledge.directoryConfig.retrievalHint: Control whether documents under this folder participate in lexical and vector retrieval.
knowledge.directoryConfig.lexicalSearchH: Controls whether documents in this folder enter the lexical index and lexical recall.
knowledge.directoryConfig.semanticSearch: Controls whether documents in this folder keep embeddings and participate in vector recall.
knowledge.directoryConfig.lexicalRuleInh: Follow the nearest parent LX rule. Default folders stay enabled.
knowledge.directoryConfig.lexicalRuleEna: LX. Add this folder's documents to the lexical index and lexical recall.
knowledge.directoryConfig.lexicalRuleDis: Disable LX. Skip lexical indexing and lexical recall for this folder.
knowledge.directoryConfig.semanticRuleIn: Follow the nearest parent SM rule. Default folders stay enabled.
knowledge.directoryConfig.semanticRuleEn: SM. Keep embeddings for this folder and include them in semantic recall.
knowledge.directoryConfig.semanticRuleDi: Disable SM. Skip embeddings and semantic recall for this folder.
knowledge.directoryConfig.ruleInheritHin: Follow the nearest parent folder rule. Default folders stay enabled.
knowledge.directoryConfig.ruleEnableHint: Keep this retrieval path enabled for the current folder.
knowledge.directoryConfig.ruleDisableHin: Turn off this retrieval path for the current folder.
knowledge.chat.title: Document Chat
knowledge.chat.subtitle: Discuss the current document and submit targeted edits
knowledge.chat.placeholder: Describe how you want to revise this document
knowledge.chat.readOnlyPlaceholder: This document is read-only. Use chat to discuss content and edit proposals
knowledge.chat.emptyHint: The chat automatically carries the current document path, type, and maintenance rules for more accurate edit proposals.
knowledge.noFilesHint: Right-click to create file or folder
knowledge.welcome.title: Welcome to Knowledge
knowledge.welcome.desc: Manage project content in your knowledge base so the Agent understands your project better
knowledge.welcome.manualDesc: Create a blank doc and write freely
knowledge.welcome.importDesc: Sync docs from external sources like Feishu
knowledge.welcome.tip1: Manage design, memory, skill, and reference docs in the left panel
knowledge.welcome.tip2: Use the search bar for semantic search (requires embedding)
knowledge.welcome.tip3: Right-click the doc list to create files or folders
knowledge.overview.title: Knowledge Overview
knowledge.overview.subtitle: Review the unified knowledge documents currently visible under {0}.
knowledge.overview.searchIndex.title: Search Index
knowledge.overview.searchIndex.hintBody: Shows lexical full-text and semantic vector search together: lexical tracks Fresh / Stale / Pending states, while semantic shows coverage and index health so you can verify retrieval readiness at a glance.
knowledge.overview.freshness.title: Knowledge Freshness
knowledge.overview.freshness.hintBody: Tracks whether indexed knowledge docs are fresh, need refresh, or still have unknown status.
knowledge.overview.freshness.emptyNote: Freshness status is not available yet. These stats will update after indexing or synchronization completes.
knowledge.overview.context.title: Context Load
knowledge.overview.context.hintBody: Estimates context pressure in bytes so you can judge how much cost knowledge injection and retrieval add to the Agent.
knowledge.overview.context.caption: Always-on · 1 token ≈ 3.5 bytes
knowledge.overview.performance.title: Storage Footprint
knowledge.overview.performance.hintBody: Shows disk usage for the index store, runtime cache, Tantivy, local model, and the average chunk count per item.
knowledge.overview.performance.caption: Total footprint · {0} chunks/item
knowledge.dashboard.knowledge.title: Knowledge Dashboard
knowledge.dashboard.knowledge.subtitle: Review knowledge docs, search index status, sources, and overall load.
knowledge.dashboard.knowledge.tokenUsage: Shows current directory injection, knowledge usage rule, maintenance rule, recent query, and recent update token estimates.
knowledge.dashboard.knowledge.currentInj: Estimated from the current knowledge directory always-on injection
knowledge.dashboard.knowledge.recentQuer: Estimated from the top 3 hits of the latest query
knowledge.dashboard.knowledge.recentUpda: Estimated from the latest doc update input
knowledge.dashboard.skill.title: Skill Dashboard
knowledge.dashboard.skill.subtitle: Review reusable workflow packs, activation surfaces, and shared retrieval runtime health.
knowledge.dashboard.skill.surfaceHint: Counts command, auto, and both surfaces based on the current enabled state.
knowledge.dashboard.skill.runtimeCaption: Skills reuse the knowledge retrieval index
knowledge.dashboard.memory.title: Memory Dashboard
knowledge.dashboard.memory.subtitle: Review agent-maintained long-term memory, context load, and maintenance token estimates.
knowledge.dashboard.memory.contextCaptio: Current session estimate for Memory injection
knowledge.dashboard.memory.contextEnable: All Memory documents are currently injected into session context.
knowledge.dashboard.memory.contextDisabl: Memory is not injected right now; enabling it will add about {0} tok.
knowledge.dashboard.memory.injectionNoti: All Memory documents are injected into context together; more files and longer content increase the prompt footprint.
knowledge.dashboard.memory.maintenanceCa: Estimated from document content, regardless of the current enabled state.
knowledge.dashboard.design.subtitle: Review project design documents co-created with the agent, along with structure, summary coverage, and recent updates.
knowledge.dashboard.memoryType.subtitle: Review agent-maintained long-term memory. Users can inspect and edit it.
knowledge.dashboard.skillType.subtitle: Review reusable workflow packs, command entry coverage, and recent updates.
knowledge.dashboard.reference.subtitle: Review locally collected external references and source distribution.
knowledge.dashboard.metadata.note: This card aggregates summary coverage, maintenance state, command entry, and read-only status for the current type.
knowledge.referenceImport.title: Unity Engine Docs
knowledge.referenceImport.subtitle: Download the official offline docs for the current project version and convert them into searchable Markdown.
knowledge.referenceImport.note.ready: The Unity docs for the current project version are available in Reference and ready for search and browsing.
knowledge.referenceImport.note.outdated: The project version changed. Reimport the matching official docs.
knowledge.referenceImport.note.unavailab: No Unity version information is available for the current workspace.
knowledge.referenceImport.note.error: The import task stopped. Refresh the status and start it again.
knowledge.referenceImport.note.running: Downloading, extracting, and converting the official Unity offline docs.
knowledge.referenceImport.note.missing: The official Unity docs have not been imported yet.
knowledge.referenceImport.startedNotice: Started importing the official Unity docs.
knowledge.referenceImport.cancelledNotic: Cancelled importing the official Unity docs.
knowledge.referenceImport.completedNotic: Unity docs import completed with {0} documents.
knowledge.referenceImport.window.title: Importing Unity Docs
knowledge.referenceImport.window.languag: Choose the language of the official Unity offline docs to download.
knowledge.referenceImport.window.setupHi: Choose a document language, then start the import.
knowledge.referenceImport.window.setupDe: This window will show the download, extraction, conversion, and indexing progress.
knowledge.referenceImport.window.autoClo: The docs were written into Reference. This window will close automatically.
The import was cancelled. This window will close automatically.
The import stopped. This window will close automatically.
knowledge.referenceImport.window.downloa: Downloading the official Unity offline docs archive.
knowledge.referenceImport.window.convert: Converting HTML into Markdown and writing it into Reference.
knowledge.referenceImport.window.reconci: Switching the managed directory and updating the retrieval index.
knowledge.referenceFolder.external.hint: Configure Feishu or Unity sources directly inside the current Reference folder. Imported content is written into this directory and the source is stored in the folder metadata.
knowledge.referenceFolder.external.overv: Choose a source and start importing here. The Reference folder is created automatically from the external source and keeps its source metadata.
knowledge.referenceFolder.external.unity: This project does not have Unity docs imported yet.
knowledge.referenceFolder.external.folde: Enter a single folder name here. The parent path comes from the current entry point.
Folder names cannot contain path separators.
knowledge.referenceFolder.external.targe: Choose a source setup to generate the target directory
knowledge.referenceFolder.external.unity: This project already has a Unity docs directory. Locus will update that location directly.
This project already imports Unity docs into {0}. Continuing will update that directory directly.
knowledge.referenceFolder.external.dialo: Choose a source first, then finish configuration and import in one place. Feishu folders are named from the selected sync scope, and Unity reuses the single project docs directory.
knowledge.referenceFolder.external.windo: Finish source configuration and import in this independent window. Import progress stays visible here.
This directory already has external sources. Update the setup and reimport in this independent window.
knowledge.feishuReference.title: Feishu Knowledge Base
knowledge.feishuReference.subtitle: Import documents from Feishu wiki spaces into a managed Reference directory for shared retrieval.
knowledge.feishuReference.cancelledNotic: Cancelled the Feishu knowledge base import.
knowledge.feishuReference.deletedNotice: Deleted the imported Feishu knowledge base content.
knowledge.feishuReference.completedNotic: Feishu knowledge base import completed with {0} documents.
knowledge.feishuReference.window.title: Feishu Knowledge Base Import
knowledge.feishuReference.window.subtitl: Config saves automatically. Test the connection, choose a space and import folders, then start the managed import.
knowledge.feishuReference.window.connect: App identity and user identity share the same Feishu app. App Secret is stored in the system keychain.
knowledge.feishuReference.window.appSecr: Enter or replace the Feishu app secret
knowledge.feishuReference.window.connect: Current identity: {0}. Config saves automatically. App identity can test and import directly, while OAuth can test the connection after authorization completes.
knowledge.feishuReference.window.persist: This authorization only covers the current session. Re-authorize after the access token expires.
This authorization requests offline_access so Locus can refresh the user_access_token automatically.
knowledge.feishuReference.window.oauthAd: One-time admin setup: add every callback URL below in Feishu Open Platform > Security Settings > Redirect URL. Missing this step triggers 20029 on the authorization page.
knowledge.feishuReference.window.oauthBi: The current authorization is bound to app {0} on {1}. Re-authorize after either value changes.
knowledge.feishuReference.window.copyCal: Failed to copy the callback URL. Copy it manually.
knowledge.feishuReference.window.missing: The current authorization is missing required scopes: {0}. Start user authorization again to grant the current scope set.
knowledge.feishuReference.window.scopeHi: Load the available spaces first, then check the folders to import in the browser.
knowledge.feishuReference.window.selectS: Test the connection first, then choose a space
Test the connection and choose a space first.
knowledge.feishuReference.window.emptyNo: No visible nodes are available at this level.
knowledge.feishuReference.window.importH: Imported content is written into a managed directory, stays read-only, and can be synced again later.
knowledge.feishuReference.window.authori: User authorization started. Current callback URL: {0}
knowledge.feishuReference.window.appSecr: This input will be saved to the system keychain after autosave completes.
The app secret is already stored in the system keychain.
No app secret is stored yet.
knowledge.feishuReference.window.authori: Wait for the current config to autosave before starting user authorization
knowledge.injectionPreview.title: Injection Preview
knowledge.injectionPreview.emptyHint: This view only shows the runtime Knowledge block that is currently injected.
knowledge.injectionPreview.noAgent: No agent is currently available for preview.
knowledge.retrieval.title: Retrieval Settings
knowledge.retrieval.subtitle: Review lexical and vector index status, control how the knowledge library is queried, and inspect current retrieval load.
knowledge.retrieval.lexicalHint: Tantivy-backed inverted index for precise recall over titles, paths, and document content.
knowledge.retrieval.semanticIdle: The vector runtime is currently inactive.
knowledge.retrieval.semanticReady: The vector runtime is ready and can participate in hybrid recall.
knowledge.retrieval.runtimeFallback: {0} failed to start. The current runtime backend has fallen back to {1}.
knowledge.retrieval.performanceHint: Review index files, database size, and runtime memory footprint.
knowledge.retrieval.queryHint: Review the latest search mode, latency, and token estimate.
knowledge.retrieval.localModelDownloaded: The current model is ready in the local model directory and can be used for semantic retrieval.
knowledge.retrieval.localModelPendingHin: The current model is not downloaded yet; enabling semantic retrieval will download it into the local model directory.
knowledge.retrieval.huggingFaceModelCust: You can enter any Hugging Face repo ID here. The repo must include ONNX and tokenizer files.
knowledge.retrieval.downloadPopoverHint: Presets are ordered from smaller to larger model sizes; you can also enter a Hugging Face repo ID that includes ONNX and tokenizer files.
knowledge.retrieval.downloadPresetHint: Supports automatic download into the local model directory.
knowledge.retrieval.downloadSourceOffici: The official endpoint is the default and fits general network conditions.
knowledge.retrieval.downloadSourceMirror: HF-Mirror is available as a manually selected alternate endpoint.
knowledge.retrieval.presetEstimateHint: VRAM and RAM are small-batch local inference estimates. Actual usage changes with provider, batch size, max length, and truncation dimension.
knowledge.retrieval.deviceAdviceGpuFit: Running on {0} with about {1} of dedicated GPU memory, so the headroom is comfortable.
knowledge.retrieval.deviceAdviceGpuTight: Running on {0} with about {1} of dedicated GPU memory. It should work, but keep batch size and max length under control.
knowledge.retrieval.deviceAdviceGpuInsuf: Running on {0} with about {1} of dedicated GPU memory. This is a tight fit, so a smaller model is safer.
knowledge.retrieval.deviceAdviceGpuUnkno: Running on {0}, but the GPU memory budget is not available yet. Plan around roughly {1}.
knowledge.retrieval.deviceAdviceCpuWithS: You are on CPU now, but the machine also has about {0} of dedicated GPU memory available. Switching to GPU should give steadier throughput.
You are on CPU now, and the available GPU headroom is limited. The CPU path is a better fit for lighter models.
knowledge.retrieval.deviceAdviceCpuOnlyL: You are on CPU now. The load is manageable for low-concurrency retrieval.
knowledge.retrieval.deviceAdviceCpuOnlyM: You are on CPU now. It is usable, but longer inputs and larger batches will show more latency.
knowledge.retrieval.deviceAdviceCpuOnlyH: You are on CPU now. The load is heavy, so a smaller model or a GPU route is the better choice.
knowledge.retrieval.preset.qwen4.summary: Main workhorse for multilingual retrieval, long context, and shrinkable dimensions.
knowledge.retrieval.preset.qwen4.officia: The Qwen model card highlights instruction-aware retrieval, recommends English instructions for multilingual usage, and suggests flash_attention_2 for better memory efficiency.
knowledge.retrieval.preset.qwen4.setup: Prefer 12GB+ GPU memory or around 24GB of system RAM. For retrieval-only workloads, truncating to 1024 or 1536 dims cuts SQLite vector cost noticeably.
knowledge.retrieval.preset.qwen06.summar: Lightweight commercial-friendly option for multilingual retrieval and shrinkable dimensions.
knowledge.retrieval.preset.qwen06.offici: The Qwen model card gives the same instruction-aware guidance, recommends English instructions for multilingual use, and suggests flash_attention_2 for acceleration and memory savings.
knowledge.retrieval.preset.qwen06.setup: Prefer 4GB+ GPU memory or 8-12GB of system RAM. It is a stable choice for permissive commercial usage, local single-node runs, and light services.
Low-cost multilingual retrieval for CPU or single-GPU deployment.
The model card recommends xformers and unpadding. Its encoder-only design lowers hardware requirements and claims about a 10x inference speed gain over older decode-only GTE variants.
Prefer CPU or 3GB+ GPU memory. It fits multilingual retrieval services, lower-cost deployments, and Apache-2.0 requirements especially well.
knowledge.retrieval.preset.jinaSmall.sum: Retrieval-first with 32K context, suited for dedicated search services.
knowledge.retrieval.preset.jinaSmall.off: The model card recommends flash-attention and provides ONNX, vLLM, TEI, and GGUF deployment paths, so local and service deployments are both straightforward.
knowledge.retrieval.preset.jinaSmall.set: Prefer 6GB+ GPU memory or 8-12GB of system RAM. It is best for dedicated retrieval services, and truncating to 512 or 768 dims helps cut storage further.
knowledge.retrieval.preset.jinaNano.summ: Best for local experiments, laptops, and low-cost services.
knowledge.retrieval.preset.jinaNano.offi: The model card includes ONNX, vLLM, and GGUF usage. Flash-attention is recommended when available, but it is not required.
knowledge.retrieval.preset.jinaNano.setu: Prefer 2GB+ GPU memory or 4-6GB of system RAM. It is the best fit for laptops, local prototyping, and low-cost services.
knowledge.retrieval.preset.qwen8.summary: Quality-first option for multilingual complex retrieval and longer inputs.
knowledge.retrieval.preset.qwen8.officia: The model card emphasizes 100+ language coverage, instruction-aware retrieval, and fully shrinkable output dimensions. It is aimed squarely at quality-first workloads.
knowledge.retrieval.preset.qwen8.setup: Prefer 24GB+ GPU memory or 32GB+ of system RAM. Use it for quality-first multilingual and long-context retrieval where the higher cost is justified.
knowledge.retrieval.preset.bgeM3.summary: Upgrade path for hybrid retrieval with dense, sparse, and multi-vector outputs.
knowledge.retrieval.preset.bgeM3.officia: The model card explicitly recommends hybrid retrieval plus reranking, and notes that reducing max_length lowers latency directly.
knowledge.retrieval.preset.bgeM3.setup: Prefer 6GB+ GPU memory or 8-12GB of system RAM. Move to it when you are ready to invest in dense, sparse, and multi-vector retrieval together.

collab.stash.unanchoredTooltip: This stash's base commit is outside the current primary graph window, so it is only shown in the sidebar. Load more history and it will return to the graph once the base becomes visible.
collab.graph.workspaceChanges: Working tree has {0} uncommitted file changes
collab.overview.unstagedHint: Review these changes before deciding what belongs in this commit
collab.overview.cleanHint: Working tree is clean, so you can focus on staged work
collab.overview.stagedHint: These changes will be included in the next commit
collab.overview.stagedEmptyHint: Stage the files you want to include next
collab.orphanMetaHint: No primary file appears in the current list, so this entry is never hidden by the .meta filter
collab.orphanMetaWarning: {0} orphan .meta files have no primary file in the current list, so they stay visible even when .meta entries are hidden
collab.blockedHint: {0} paths are blocked by platform rules. Stage All will continue with the remaining changes.
collab.blockedReason.windowsTrailingDot: Path segment ends with a dot: {0}
collab.blockedReason.windowsTrailingSpac: Path segment ends with a space: {0}
collab.stageAllCompatibleTitle: Stage all changes that are compatible with this platform
collab.stageAllBlockedOnly: {0} skipped, no compatible changes can be staged on this platform
collab.discardMsgUntracked: This will permanently delete this untracked file.
collab.discardMsgSingle: This will restore the file to its last committed state. All uncommitted changes will be lost.
collab.discardMsgMulti: This will discard all changes in {0} files. Untracked files will be deleted. All uncommitted changes will be lost.
collab.discardLocusWarnSingle: This file is Locus-managed content. Discarding it will remove the related knowledge, memory, or config changes.
collab.discardLocusWarnMulti: The selection includes {0} Locus-managed files. Discarding them will remove the related knowledge, memory, or config changes.
collab.resetHardMsg: The current branch will be moved to this commit. All uncommitted changes will be lost.
collab.dropStashMsg: This stash will be permanently removed.
collab.deleteBranchMsg: This branch will be removed from the local repository.

merge.back.title: Back to conflict list
merge.banner.structuredPending: Choices in the structured view are staged. Click "Apply structured result" to write them to the file.
merge.banner.stashMeaning: Applying a stash uses a three-way comparison: Current changes are the file contents you are applying onto now, Stashed changes are the changes recorded in the stash, and Shared base is Git's common starting point for comparing and merging both sides. Here, "Updated upstream" means the current side, not a remote upstream branch.
merge.banner.externalChanges: The file changed outside Locus. Block picking is disabled until you resolve it manually below.
merge.footer.applyHint: Write the staged structured choices to the file.
merge.footer.applyBlocked: Choose a source for every unresolved field before applying.
merge.queue.allResolvedBanner: All conflict files are resolved. Click Continue to finish.
merge.queue.selectFileHint: Select a file from the list to continue resolving structured or text conflicts.
merge.queue.abortMessage: This discards the current conflict-resolution progress and restores the working directory to its pre-operation state.
merge.panels.selectTarget: Select an object to review structured conflicts.
merge.panels.selectionHint: Click a source value to choose which side this field should use.
merge.panels.noFields: No structured fields to show in this section.
merge.leave.title: Discard pending choices?
merge.leave.message: You have structured choices that have not been applied yet. Leave this file and lose those staged choices?
diff.optimized.allFiltered: These fields are hidden in Simplified View. Switch to Full View to inspect everything.
agent.guide.title: View Agent Context
agent.guide.desc: Select an item on the left to view soul.md or edit rules
agent.dashboard.title: System Prompt Dashboard
agent.dashboard.desc: Estimates baseline context, tool definitions, and runtime injections from the active agent and workspace.
agent.dashboard.healthSummary.healthy: The current structure is compact and suits steady conversations and routine retrieval injection.
agent.dashboard.healthSummary.watch: Size and distribution are still under control; keep an eye on new rules and injected context.
agent.dashboard.healthSummary.heavy: Prompt load is high; trimming rules and runtime context will keep it more stable.
agent.dashboard.footerMeta: Includes system prompt, env, rules, knowledge injection, and tool schemas. Chat CTX will still add session history, current-turn output, and cache read/write.
agent.dashboard.note.total.light: Baseline context is light and leaves plenty of headroom for routine tasks.
agent.dashboard.note.total.steady: Baseline context is steady and fits ongoing chat plus retrieval injection.
agent.dashboard.note.total.heavy: Baseline context is high; trimming rules, runtime context, or tools should be the first step.
agent.dashboard.note.rules.active: {0}/{1} rules are enabled, so the rule layer is in place.
agent.dashboard.note.rules.empty: No rules are currently enabled, so behavior constraints are weaker.
agent.dashboard.note.distribution.balanc: The composition stays balanced without a single inflated section.
agent.dashboard.note.distribution.domina: The current load is concentrated in {0}.
agent.dashboard.note.knowledge.light: Runtime context load is still light and leaves room for more retrieval results.
agent.dashboard.note.knowledge.heavy: Runtime context already takes a large share, so more injected knowledge will raise cost quickly.
agent.noAgent.title: Select an Agent
agent.noAgent.desc: Select an Agent on the left to view its soul.md and rule config
agent.tool.noParameters: This tool does not define any parameters.
